title: Real spectator seating - backend and domain foundation

scope.frontend_wiring: NOT DONE - deliberately out of scope for this command

authoritative_discovery.capacity_derivation: sum(SPECTATOR_RING_SEGMENTS); not equal per ring - the front row holds 40 and the back row 96
authoritative_discovery.seat_order_source: get_arena_geometry() ring order (row 1 nearest the rail first), then cell index ascending within each ring - the same order the existing renderer already fills seats in (static/js/arena_render.js assignSpectators)
authoritative_discovery.existing_presence_system: chef_battle/models.py :: BattleViewerPresence (pseudonymised device hash, no account linkage) - kept as the audience counter, not extended into seating
authoritative_discovery.existing_online_window: selectors.ARENA_ONLINE_THRESHOLD_SECONDS = 180, already the arena's definition of who is present
authoritative_discovery.prior_seating_behaviour: views._get_spectators returned online authors ordered by -last_seen_at and the renderer poured them into seats in list order; no seat was owned, so a viewer's seat moved on every poll

implementation.model: chef_battle.models.ArenaSeat (viewer FK -> recipes.RecipeAuthor, ring_index, seat_index, claimed_at, released_at)
implementation.service: chef_battle/arena_seating.py (seat_map, seating_capacity, get_active_seat, claim_seat, release_seat, public_seat)
implementation.endpoint: POST /chef-battle/arena/seat/ -> chef_battle:arena_take_seat (login required, dark-launch gated, rate limited 60/m per IP)
database_constraints: unique_active_arena_seat_per_viewer - UNIQUE(viewer) WHERE released_at IS NULL
unique_active_occupant_per_arena_seat - UNIQUE(ring_index, seat_index) WHERE released_at IS NULL
implementation.concurrency_strategy: optimistic: read the free-seat map, insert, let the partial unique constraint reject the loser, retry against a refreshed map; retry budget is the whole hall
implementation.idempotency: claim_seat returns the viewer's existing held seat; the IntegrityError path also re-checks for the caller's own seat before retrying
implementation.lifecycle: explicit release, plus a lapse when the holder falls outside the existing 180s arena online window. No new TTL, heartbeat or expiry clock was invented.
implementation.synthetic_occupants: impossible - viewer is a non-null FK to a real author and there is no factory, fixture, seeding command or service path that creates a seat for anything else

privacy.rationale: identical to the three public author fields the arena payload has always carried for a spectator (views._get_spectators), plus the seat coordinates

remaining_work_for_owner_decision: The renderer still seats spectators from the transient _get_spectators list; wiring the arena payload and arena_render.js to ArenaSeat is presentation work and was excluded from this command.
Whether a viewer may choose a specific free seat (the renderer already marks seats data-seatable) or only be assigned the front-most one. Only assignment is implemented.
Whether enrolled chefs, who sit in their rank ring rather than the stands, should also hold an ArenaSeat row.
A migration will run on production when this eventually deploys: one new table, no data migration, reversible.
